fresh_facts.claim: President Trump announced on April 14, 2026 that the US military has initiated a blockade of all Iranian ports to pressure Iran into reopening the Strait of Hormuz.
The US Navy blockade of Iranian ports began on April 13, 2026, covering the Strait of Hormuz, Gulf of Oman, and Arabian Sea.
Trump stated that Iran's navy, air force, anti-aircraft systems, and radar have been neutralized as part of military operations.
Iran's Strait of Hormuz closure has lasted six weeks as of April 14, 2026, with only Iranian or friendly vessels allowed passage for fees.
Vessel transits through the Strait dropped from 100+ daily pre-war to approximately 40 post-ceasefire as of April 14, 2026.
The IRGC warned of mine-littered danger zones and potential small boat missile attacks on US ships in response to the blockade.
Iran threatened retaliation against all Persian Gulf and Gulf of Oman ports, including US allied ports.
Nuclear talks between the US and Iran in Islamabad collapsed without agreement over Iran's nuclear program on the weekend before April 14, 2026.
fresh_facts.source: Iran state media and news reports

fresh_facts.claim: Pakistan acted as a facilitator in failed US-Iran nuclear talks due to its ties to the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps.
Iran is weighing a pause in Strait of Hormuz shipments to avoid derailing diplomatic talks while maintaining a defiant message.
The Mossad chief stated that the mission in Iran is not over until regime change occurs.
An Israeli security insider claimed Iranians are weeks away from another uprising as of April 12, 2026.
Over 1,000 hours of internet shutdowns have caused at least $35 million in daily losses to small businesses and startups.
Petrochemical layoffs and factory closures are widespread in Iran amid deepening economic crisis.
Severe medicine shortages and price spikes are exacerbating a healthcare crisis in Iran following European powers' snapback sanctions in August 2025.
Iranian authorities executed 14 people in 2025-2026, with 7 linked to protests, as part of 1,639 total executions in 2025, the highest since 1989.
A 41-day internet blackout persists in Iran with armed security deployed on streets as of April 12, 2026.
The Iranian regime is described as harder line than ever, with effective instruments of state control including arrests, torture, and imprisonment against demonstrations.
Recent Iranian protests represent the most serious unrest ever faced by the regime, threatening its character.
The US revoked green cards of three Iranians tied to the regime and placed them in custody on Saturday before April 13, 2026.
Brent crude oil is nearing $100 per barrel due to Iran's Strait of Hormuz closure as of April 14, 2026.
France's Macron urged the US and Iran to resume talks and halt escalation.
Israel announced direct talks with Lebanon starting the following week in Washington, conditional on Israeli de-escalation.
Lebanon is pressing for a temporary ceasefire in negotiations with Israel.
The fragile US-Iran ceasefire masks unresolved nuclear ambitions, Lebanon destabilization, rising terrorism risks, and shadow war potential according to CSIS analysis.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces escalating multi-front pressure from US naval blockade (initiated April 13, 2026), collapsed nuclear diplomacy, severe internal crackdowns with record executions and 41-day internet blackouts, and coordinated international isolation including Israeli regime change objectives and French de-escalation calls. Economic deterioration accelerates amid petrochemical layoffs, medicine shortages, and $35 million daily losses from internet shutdowns, while the Strait of Hormuz closure drives Brent crude toward $100/barrel.
regime_fall.last_major_event: President Trump announced on April 14, 2026 that the US military has initiated a comprehensive blockade of all Iranian ports, with claims that Iranian naval, air, and air defense capabilities have been neutralized, following collapsed nuclear talks and a six-week Strait of Hormuz closure.
